questions.scenario: Users call `orders.devpolaris.com` through a load balancer. Which certificate matters to the browser?

options.text: The certificate served by the public entry point for `orders.devpolaris.com`.
Any certificate in the source repository.

questions.explanation: The client validates the certificate presented at the public entry it reaches.

questions.scenario: The backend container listens on an internal HTTP port behind an HTTPS load balancer. What is the healthy interpretation?

options.text: TLS can terminate at the public entry while the backend uses an internal protocol path.
HTTPS is impossible unless the container itself owns the public certificate.

questions.explanation: Public HTTPS is often handled at the load balancer or service entry, not necessarily inside the container.

questions.scenario: Which checks belong in HTTPS entry review?

options.text: The certificate covers the public name.
The public entry point serves that certificate.
The certificate is not expired and can renew.
The subnet name contains the word secure.

questions.explanation: HTTPS review follows the name, certificate, serving entry, and renewal state.
